Identity.History: Hippocrates of Kos lived around 460–370 BC and became the most famous physician of ancient Greece. Later tradition called him the “Father of Medicine” because he and his followers promoted careful observation, diagnosis, prognosis, and natural explanations for illness rather than blaming disease on divine punishment. The Hippocratic Corpus, a collection of medical writings linked to his school, shaped Greek and later Roman medicine. The Hippocratic Oath, though probably not written by him personally, preserves his enduring association with medical ethics.
